visualGoal: Frame head tracking as the cheapest upgrade that turns a flat monitor into a window, then settle the clip-vs-clip-free decision by what you fly. Real product proof of both units; no procedural art.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic home flight-sim desk at a single flat monitor showing an MSFS cockpit banking into a turn, warm gold rim light on deep blue-grey walls, a pilot leaning to look into the turn, open product-overlay space on top of and beneath the monitor edge for a real head-tracking camera and a real sensor bar, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake hardware, warped monitor, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: TrackIR 5 vs Tobii Eye Tracker 5
content: TrackIR: IR camera watches a clip you wear, ~$174, sub-mm/~9 ms, unaffected by glasses/beards, best for fighters/warbirds/DCS
Tobii: sensor bar watches head+eyes, nothing to wear, ~$339, native MSFS 2020/2024, best for GA/airliners on one screen
Counterintuitive: the eye tracker is the more expensive option
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim two-column comparison card, large readable text, flight blues/golds, real product photos as column headers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Which head tracker: the buy order
content: 2 Weight by what you fly (fast = TrackIR, GA = Tobii)
3 Check monitor (single <=30 in keeps Tobii in play)
5 Sort lighting + neutral seat, bind recenter
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a single-monitor flight desk, wearing an aviation headset with a small reflective clip, one hand gesturing at the monitor's head-tracking camera, confident pilot-instructor energy, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: MSFS cockpit view demonstrating look-around freedom (looking into a turn / clearing the nose on a taildragger)
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Optional supporting visual for the 'monitor becomes a window' concept. Must be a genuine MSFS capture, not fabricated UI. Low priority.

openQuestions: Only two products in the comparison — add Codex mechanism/at-a-glance infographics to lift visual density toward roundup level, or accept the lower density for a two-unit comparison?
Re-confirm both street prices (~$174 TrackIR, ~$339 / ~$289 refurb Tobii) at certification time — they move with sales?
